title: Daily Briefing — Mar 18
headline: Tech and financials cracking at the 20 MA, volatility rising, flows warn: Don't chase this drop.
body: Right, so let's look at the flows. Markets are rolling over sharply—SPY at $661.58 is now well below its 20 MA ($678.96), and the VIX has jumped to 24.39, trending above the 20 MA. Volatility is expanding and breadth is thinning, classic mean reversion territory. Most of big tech—AAPL, MSFT, NVDA—are breaking down below daily 20 MAs, and the indices are confirming a bearish bias.

Sector rotation's flashing new signals: Energy (XLE, $58.46) remains firmly leading and low risk, while Industrials (XLI, $165.13) cling to leadership, but everything else is sliding into lagging or weakening quadrants. Financials (XLF, $48.96) show elevated risk—approaching daily 20 MA support, 'the canary' is not chirping. Tech is still improving on RRG, but price action says otherwise; XLK ($138.02) flirting with its 20 MA ($139.40), watch the level like a hawk.

Contrarian warning: No extreme sentiment triggers—Fear & Greed is neutral at 48, so no panic or greed play today—but the daily close below the 20 MA (R009) triggers defensive posture. VIX above 20 means risk-off until flows confirm support. Don't chase—abundance mindset. Record retail demand is nowhere here, but financials breaking support is the macro risk flag. If bonds freak out (HYG/LQD both breaking lower), everything breaks; we're seeing early tremors, so keep your finger near the eject button.
callout_body:
SPY: $661.58 (below 20 MA $678.96, mean reversion zone)
QQQ: $594.92 (below 20 MA $604.83, watch for weekly reversion)
HYG: $79.42 (approaching SMA20 $80.06, risk when bonds break)
XLF: $48.96 (approaching SMA20 $50.68, financials weakness = macro warning)
XLK: $138.02 (watch SMA20 $139.40 for tech mean reversion)
XLE: $58.46 (leading, active rotation play)
VIX: 24.39 (elevated, risk-off regime)
action_items: Reduce exposure to equities trading below their daily 20 MA—especially SPY, QQQ, XLK, XLF per rule R009.
Look for possible rotational entry in Energy (XLE) as sector remains leading with low risk and strong price above MAs.
closing_quote: Patience, react, don’t predict—if it’s in the press, it’s in the price.
